title: The Assembly Appropriations Committee passed a package of bills by Senator Dave Cortese on Friday, including Senate Bill (SB) 10 to prevent opioid overdoses at public schools. SB 1
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: SB 10 requires that school safety plans include protocols for responding to pupils suffering from an opioid overdose.
SB 10 aims to equip schools with knowledge and tools to save children who overdose on campus.
The bill ensures that every middle and high school in California can access and afford the opioid antagonist Naloxone, also known as Narcan.
Studies show that religious practices reduce violence and other negative behaviors in correctional facilities and lower recidivism.
